2026-04-17T15:06:59.8987264Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8987840Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8988262Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8988623Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8988933Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8989167Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8989325Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8989508Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8989678Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8989906Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8990108Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8990393Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T15:06:59.8990655Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). 2026-04-17T15:09:56.3000974Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T15:08:08.745267119Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58293,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware and new Responses logging features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary); also `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58274 added the following features to the AgentServer packages:\\n\\n1. **`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`** (Core, `beta.22`) — a new auto-registered ASP.NET Core middleware that logs every inbound HTTP request with method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`, OpenTelemetry trace ID). Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available.\\n3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. It only says traces come from the Responses/Invocations activity sources, which is now incomplete and misleading — users may not realize all HTTP requests are automatically logged.\\n- The **Core README Key concepts section** does not list `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` or describe the built-in HTTP-level logging as a default server behavior.\\n- The **Responses README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`) does not mention handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, leaving users unable to discover what log categories/levels to configure.\\n- The **Responses README Key concepts section** does not document `agent_session_id` derivation — developers building conversation-aware handlers need to understand that session IDs are deterministically derived from agent name, version, and partition hint, and what the fallback behavior is.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Health endpoint`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Request logging\\n\\n`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically and logs every inbound HTTP request\\nto the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` logger category:\\n\\n| Event | Log level | Fields |\\n|-------|-----------|--------|\\n| Request started | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` |\\n| Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `StatusCode`, `DurationMs`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` |\\n| Request failed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | Same as above |\\n\\nQuery strings are intentionally excluded from all log messages to avoid logging sensitive data.\\nTo adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Warning\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured\\n`Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for\\n4xx/5xx responses. Correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) and the\\nOpenTelemetry trace ID are included in every log entry for cross-service request tracing.\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your\\napplication configuration to diagnose startup issues.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Thread safety`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID derivation\\n\\nEach response is assigned a deterministic `agent_session_id` derived from a SHA-256 hash of\\n`\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters.\\nThe partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the\\nrequest. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead.\\n\\nThe derivation priority is:\\n1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload\\n2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable\\n3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available)\\n4. Random 63-char hex (fallback)\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. Each log\\nentry includes the `ResponseId` and, where applicable, `ConversationId`, `PreviousResponseId`,\\n`Model`, `Streaming`, `Background`, `Store`, `Status`, and `OutputCount` fields:\\n\\n| Endpoint | Log details |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| POST `/responses` | response ID, model, streaming, background, store, conversation ID, previous response ID |\\n| POST `/responses` (completed, non-streaming) | final status and output count |\\n| GET `/responses/{id}` | retrieved status and output count |\\n| POST `/responses/{id}/cancel` | result status |\\n| DELETE `/responses/{id}` | success flag |\\n| GET `/responses/{id}/input_items` | response ID |\\n\\nThe `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information`\\nlevel with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers.\\n\\nOpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose storage and handler issues.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these documentation-only additions. All content is conceptual description and configuration examples that do not need snippet extraction.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs new subsections added** as described in Step 1\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **Logging subsections need updating** as described in Step 1\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n6. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export is needed since these are documentation-only changes.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-17T15:09:56.3032266Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T15:08:08.759488299Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58293,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T15:09:56.3068736Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T15:08:08.969945991Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58293,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware and new Responses logging features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary); also `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58274 added the following features to the AgentServer packages:\\n\\n1. **`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`** (Core, `beta.22`) — a new auto-registered ASP.NET Core middleware that logs every inbound HTTP request with method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`, OpenTelemetry trace ID). Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available.\\n3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. It only says traces come from the Responses/Invocations activity sources, which is now incomplete and misleading — users may not realize all HTTP requests are automatically logged.\\n- The **Core README Key concepts section** does not list `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` or describe the built-in HTTP-level logging as a default server behavior.\\n- The **Responses README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`) does not mention handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, leaving users unable to discover what log categories/levels to configure.\\n- The **Responses README Key concepts section** does not document `agent_session_id` derivation — developers building conversation-aware handlers need to understand that session IDs are deterministically derived from agent name, version, and partition hint, and what the fallback behavior is.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Health endpoint`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Request logging\\n\\n`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically and logs every inbound HTTP request\\nto the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` logger category:\\n\\n| Event | Log level | Fields |\\n|-------|-----------|--------|\\n| Request started | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` |\\n| Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `StatusCode`, `DurationMs`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` |\\n| Request failed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | Same as above |\\n\\nQuery strings are intentionally excluded from all log messages to avoid logging sensitive data.\\nTo adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Warning\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured\\n`Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for\\n4xx/5xx responses. Correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) and the\\nOpenTelemetry trace ID are included in every log entry for cross-service request tracing.\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your\\napplication configuration to diagnose startup issues.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Thread safety`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID derivation\\n\\nEach response is assigned a deterministic `agent_session_id` derived from a SHA-256 hash of\\n`\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters.\\nThe partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the\\nrequest. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead.\\n\\nThe derivation priority is:\\n1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload\\n2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable\\n3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available)\\n4. Random 63-char hex (fallback)\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. Each log\\nentry includes the `ResponseId` and, where applicable, `ConversationId`, `PreviousResponseId`,\\n`Model`, `Streaming`, `Background`, `Store`, `Status`, and `OutputCount` fields:\\n\\n| Endpoint | Log details |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| POST `/responses` | response ID, model, streaming, background, store, conversation ID, previous response ID |\\n| POST `/responses` (completed, non-streaming) | final status and output count |\\n| GET `/responses/{id}` | retrieved status and output count |\\n| POST `/responses/{id}/cancel` | result status |\\n| DELETE `/responses/{id}` | success flag |\\n| GET `/responses/{id}/input_items` | response ID |\\n\\nThe `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information`\\nlevel with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers.\\n\\nOpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose storage and handler issues.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these documentation-only additions. All content is conceptual description and configuration examples that do not need snippet extraction.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs new subsections added** as described in Step 1\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **Logging subsections need updating** as described in Step 1\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n6. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export is needed since these are documentation-only changes.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
